tool.name: Anvi'o: Generate contigs database
tool.command:
#if $contigs_fasta.is_of_type('fasta.gz') #set $input_fasta = 'input.fa.gz' #else #set $input_fasta = 'input.fa' #end if ln -s '$contigs_fasta' '$input_fasta' && #if $gene_calling.external_gene_calls ln -s '$gene_calling.external_gene_calls' 'external_gene_calls.txt' && #end if #if $description ln -s '$description' 'description.txt' && #end if mkdir -p '$contigs_db.extra_files_path' && anvi-gen-contigs-database -f '$input_fasta' -o '$contigs_db.extra_files_path/CONTIGS.db' --project-name '$project_name' -T \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} --kmer-size '$advanced.kmer_size' --split-length '$advanced.split_length' $advanced.skip_gene_calling $advanced.skip_mindful_splitting #if not $advanced.skip_gene_calling $gene_calling.prodigal_single_mode #if $gene_calling.prodigal_translation_table --prodigal-translation-table '$gene_calling.prodigal_translation_table' #end if #if $gene_calling.external_gene_calls --external-gene-calls 'external_gene_calls.txt' $gene_calling.ignore_internal_stop_codons $gene_calling.skip_predict_frame #end if #end if #if $description --description 'description.txt' #end if && printf '\n\n \n Anvi'o contigs database\n \n \n

Anvi'o contigs database

\n

This Galaxy dataset contains an Anvi'o contigs database.

\n
    \n
  • CONTIGS.db
    • \n
\n \n\n' > '$contigs_db'

param.name: contigs_fasta
param.label: Contigs FASTA
param.help: Input FASTA file containing one or more contig sequences.

param.name: project_name
param.label: Project name
param.help: Short descriptive name for this contigs database.

validator.message: Use only letters, numbers, underscores, dots, or hyphens.

param.name: description
param.label: Project description (optional)
param.help: Plain text file (Markdown supported) shown in Anvi'o interfaces and summary outputs.

param.name: prodigal_single_mode
param.label: Prodigal single-genome mode
param.help: Use Prodigal in single-genome mode instead of metagenome mode. Recommended when the input is an isolated genome rather than metagenomic contigs.

param.name: prodigal_translation_table
param.label: Prodigal translation table
param.help: NCBI translation table number for Prodigal gene calling (e.g. 4 for Mycoplasma). Leave blank to use Prodigal's default.

param.name: external_gene_calls
param.label: External gene calls (optional)
param.help: TAB-delimited file with custom gene calls. Required columns: gene_callers_id, contig, start, stop, direction, partial, call_type, source, version. Optional column: aa_sequence.

param.name: ignore_internal_stop_codons
param.label: Ignore internal stop codons in external gene calls
param.help: Only relevant when providing external gene calls. Replaces internal stop codons with X in amino acid sequences instead of raising an error.

param.name: skip_predict_frame
param.label: Skip frame prediction for external gene calls
param.help: Only relevant when providing external gene calls. Disables the Markov-model-based frame correction and translates sequences exactly as defined by start/stop positions.

param.name: kmer_size
param.label: K-mer size
param.help: Default is 4. Standard Anvi'o installations should not use values above 5.

param.name: split_length
param.label: Split length
param.help: Contigs longer than this value are soft-split by Anvi'o. Set to 0 or a negative value to disable splitting entirely.

param.name: skip_gene_calling
param.label: Skip gene calling
param.help: Do not run Prodigal. Gene calling options above are ignored when this is enabled.

param.name: skip_mindful_splitting
param.label: Skip mindful splitting
param.help: By default Anvi'o avoids splitting contigs in the middle of genes. This flag disables that behaviour.

tool.help:
Generate an Anvi'o contigs database from a FASTA file.
This wrapper runs::
anvi-gen-contigs-database -f contigs.fa -o CONTIGS.db
The Galaxy output is typed as ``anvio_contigs_db`` so downstream Anvi'o wrappers can require this specific datatype rather than accepting an untyped binary or generic SQLite file.
Anvi'o stores key information about the input sequences in the contigs database, including k-mer frequencies, contig splits, and gene calls (via Prodigal) unless gene calling is skipped or overridden with external calls.
**Gene calling options**
By default Anvi'o runs Prodigal in metagenome mode. Use *single-genome mode* when the input is an isolated genome. If you already have gene predictions, supply them as an *external gene calls* file and Anvi'o will skip Prodigal entirely for those sequences.
**Split length**
Anvi'o soft-splits long contigs into smaller pieces to improve visualisation resolution. The default is 20,000 bp. Set to 0 or a negative value to disable splitting.

citations.citation: @article{Eren2020, title = {Community-led, integrated, reproducible multi-omics with anvi'o}, volume = {6}, ISSN = {2058-5276}, url = {http://dx.doi.org/10.1038/s41564-020-00834-3}, DOI = {10.1038/s41564-020-00834-3}, number = {1}, journal = {Nature Microbiology}, publisher = {Springer Science and Business Media LLC}, author = {Eren, A. Murat and Kiefl, Evan and Shaiber, Alon and Veseli, Iva and Miller, Samuel E. and Schechter, Matthew S. and Fink, Isaac and Pan, Jessica N. and Yousef, Mahmoud and Fogarty, Emily C. and Trigodet, Florian and Watson, Andrea R. and Esen, \"{O}zcan C. and Moore, Ryan M. and Clayssen, Quentin and Lee, Michael D. and Kivenson, Veronika and Graham, Elaina D. and Merrill, Bryan D. and Karkman, Antti and Blankenberg, Daniel and Eppley, John M. and Sj\"{o}din, Andreas and Scott, Jarrod J. and Vázquez-Campos, Xabier and McKay, Luke J. and McDaniel, Elizabeth A. and Stevens, Sarah L. R. and Anderson, Rika E. and Fuessel, Jessika and Fernandez-Guerra, Antonio and Maignien, Lois and Delmont, Tom O. and Willis, Amy D.}, year = {2020}, month = Dec, pages = {3–6} }
